title: BTC ML Model: +13,000 Points in Last 12 Signals (60 Days) — Sharing My Approach
author: Spirited_Syllabub488
selftext:
I’ve been running a live BTC machine learning model for the past two months, and over the last 12 signals (≈60 days), it has generated about +13,000 points.

A few information on its nature:

* Trades are typically held for \~4 days.
* Entry and exit occur at fixed, predefined times — no chasing price action.
* No hard stop-loss or take-profit levels; performance is managed via net results and calculated risk.
* Avoids unnecessary leverage, minimizing the risk of capital blow-up even if trades move against the position.



**“If the model is profitable, why share it?"**  
Simple — while it’s consistently performing, I’m not at the capital scale where the returns alone translate into a luxury lifestyle. Growth through compounding takes time. By letting others tap into it for a small fee, I can accelerate that journey and scale alongside the system — without compromising its integrity.

This isn’t about hype — it’s about showing that a disciplined, systematic approach to BTC trading can actually hold up in practice, without overleverage or gimmicks.

**No strategy wins every single month, but this one hasn’t posted a losing quarter in the past three years. You really need 2–3 months of live trading to see how it plays out — and how it even outperforms SPY over time.**



If anyone’s curious about the methodology or results, I’m happy to discuss.

Also users can see the stats here - [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yZGuFUf8XecgE2kel1zahbt6JrvzUeBR5LrxyOvYOyg/edit?usp=drive\_link](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yZGuFUf8XecgE2kel1zahbt6JrvzUeBR5LrxyOvYOyg/edit?usp=drive_link)

comments.author: Odd-Translator-4181
comments.body: I like how you explained the model in terms of compounding and scaling, that’s usually missing in most trading posts. For anyone who finds pure ML setups a bit overwhelming, I’d suggest also testing structured providers like UnitedKings. They’re not automated in the same way, but the predefined risk parameters can be a good complement to AI signals
